present_in_all.canonical_description: An Association relation must connect exactly one Actor to one Use Case.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: An Association relation must connect exactly one Actor to one Use Case.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Association relations connect only an Actor classifier to a Use Case classifier.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Association relations may only link Actor elements to Use Case elements; no other element types are valid endpoints.

present_in_all.canonical_description: An «extend» relationship must originate from an extending Use Case and target a base Use Case.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: An «extend» relationship must originate from an extending Use Case and target a base Use Case.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Extend relations must originate from an extending Use Case and target a base Use Case for conditional or optional behavior.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Extend relations must connect an extending Use Case to an extended Use Case, indicating optional or conditional behavior insertion.

present_in_all.canonical_description: A Generalization relation must connect two Actors or two Use Cases and not mix classifier types.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: A Generalization relation must connect two Actors or two Use Cases and not mix classifier types.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Generalization relations link classifiers of the same kind, either Actor-to-Actor or Use Case-to-Use Case.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Generalization relations are permitted only between elements of the same type (Actor→Actor or Use Case→Use Case); cross-type generalization is invalid.

present_in_all.canonical_description: A System Boundary fragment must enclose only Use Case elements and must not contain Actor elements.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: A System Boundary fragment must enclose only Use Case elements and must not contain Actor elements.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Actor classifiers must be located outside any System Boundary fragment.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: System Boundary fragments must enclose Use Case elements to define scope; Actor elements must be depicted outside any System Boundary.

present_in_multiple.canonical_description: A Use Case Diagram may contain only Actor and Use Case classifiers as elements.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: A Use Case Diagram may contain only Actor and Use Case classifiers as elements.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Use Case Diagrams may include only Actor and Use Case classifiers.

present_in_multiple.canonical_description: An «include» relationship must originate from a base Use Case and target a distinct Use Case.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: An «include» relationship must originate from a base Use Case and target a distinct Use Case.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Include relations must originate from a base Use Case and target another Use Case for mandatory shared behavior.

File 1: language_template.json.canonical_description: A Use Case may be enclosed by multiple System Boundary fragments when modeling different subjects.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: A Use Case may be enclosed by multiple System Boundary fragments when modeling different subjects.

File 2: language_template.json.canonical_description: Use Case classifiers must be enclosed within at least one System Boundary fragment.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Use Case classifiers must be enclosed within at least one System Boundary fragment.

File 2: language_template.json.canonical_description: A System Boundary fragment defines a subject and must group only Use Case classifiers relevant to that subject.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: A System Boundary fragment defines a subject and must group only Use Case classifiers relevant to that subject.

File 3: language_template.json.canonical_description: Every Actor element must have at least one Association relation to a Use Case element.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Every Actor element must have at least one Association relation to a Use Case element.

File 3: language_template.json.canonical_description: Every Use Case element must participate in at least one relationship: Association, Include, Extend, or Generalization.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Every Use Case element must participate in at least one relationship: Association, Include, Extend, or Generalization.

File 3: language_template.json.canonical_description: Include relations must connect a base Use Case to an included Use Case, indicating mandatory reuse; inclusion cycles are prohibited.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Include relations must connect a base Use Case to an included Use Case, indicating mandatory reuse; inclusion cycles are prohibited.

C1.canonical_description: A Use Case Diagram may contain only Actor and Use Case classifiers as elements.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.1: A Use Case Diagram may contain only Actor and Use Case classifiers as elements.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Use Case Diagrams may include only Actor and Use Case classifiers.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Association relations may only link Actor elements to Use Case elements; no other element types are valid endpoints.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Include relations must originate from a base Use Case and target another Use Case for mandatory shared behavior.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Extend relations must originate from an extending Use Case and target a base Use Case for conditional or optional behavior.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Extend relations must connect an extending Use Case to an extended Use Case, indicating optional or conditional behavior insertion.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Generalization relations link classifiers of the same kind, either Actor-to-Actor or Use Case-to-Use Case.
original_descriptions_by_file_index.3: Generalization relations are permitted only between elements of the same type (Actor→Actor or Use Case→Use Case); cross-type generalization is invalid.

original_descriptions_by_file_index.2: Actor classifiers must be located outside any System Boundary fragment.
